students.name: Lily Carter
students.face_management_style: tries to stay reasonable and easy to help, even when hurt
students.help_seeking_barrier: hesitates to ask directly for emotional understanding unless the other person has already shown clear attunement
students.disagreement_style: softens disagreement by sounding appreciative first and naming the problem only partially
misattunement_triggers: being compared to more assertive peers
advice that skips over her emotional effort
students.stress_masking_style: sounds cooperative and composed while quietly narrowing what she reveals
students.interaction_risk: may appear cooperative while quietly pulling away after a small relational miss

students.name: Anna Reed
students.face_management_style: tries not to look incompetent, difficult, or underprepared
students.help_seeking_barrier: will ask for help more easily on technical details than on confusion, panic, or shame
students.disagreement_style: rarely rejects guidance directly and instead asks narrower follow-up questions
misattunement_triggers: fast-paced advice
feedback delivered without reassurance about competence
students.stress_masking_style: becomes extra formal, tidy, and self-correcting when overloaded
students.interaction_risk: may become more formal and compliant instead of admitting confusion or panic

students.name: Mason Cole
students.expression_style: calm on the surface, concise, avoids burdening others
students.face_management_style: tries to look low-maintenance and self-managing
students.help_seeking_barrier: can discuss logistics or workload before he can admit emotional strain or dependence
students.disagreement_style: redirects toward practical details instead of openly saying a response missed him
misattunement_triggers: well-meaning platitudes
questions that feel too personal too early
students.stress_masking_style: converts distress into scheduling, money, or workflow language
students.interaction_risk: may redirect everything into practical details and hide the emotional stakes

students.name: Iris Bennett
students.expression_style: socially smooth, lightly joking, evasive under real stress
students.face_management_style: keeps the interaction upbeat so no one feels she is becoming heavy or messy
students.help_seeking_barrier: often waits for someone to look past the polished version before saying what is actually hard
students.disagreement_style: lets the first response pass, then vents or clarifies later in a softer way
misattunement_triggers: overly procedural responses
taking her jokes at face value
students.stress_masking_style: sounds lively, quick, and socially fluent when she is nearing overload
students.interaction_risk: can sound lively and manageable even when she is close to emotional overload

students.name: Zoe Parker
students.face_management_style: tries to sound teachable and appreciative even when she feels ashamed
students.help_seeking_barrier: finds it easier to ask for clarification than to admit that feedback intensified shame
students.disagreement_style: signals mismatch through hesitation and qualified wording rather than direct pushback
misattunement_triggers: brisk instruction
feedback that sounds like she simply needs to try harder
students.stress_masking_style: gives the correct social response first, then privately spirals afterward
students.interaction_risk: may say the right things in session while feeling more ashamed afterwards

students.name: Noah Hayes
students.expression_style: quiet, flat when tired, more open in one-to-one settings
students.face_management_style: tries not to create friction by asking for less stimulation or more time than others expect
students.help_seeking_barrier: often needs to settle first before he can explain what is actually overloading him
coping_pattern: opens up only after trust builds
students.disagreement_style: goes shorter and flatter rather than directly arguing when something feels off
misattunement_triggers: being pushed to talk before settling
having low energy mistaken for indifference
students.stress_masking_style: looks detached and minimal when he is actually overstimulated and protecting bandwidth
students.interaction_risk: can look detached or unmotivated when he is actually overwhelmed and depleted

students.name: Ethan Brooks
students.face_management_style: tries to sound rational and composed rather than openly disappointed
students.help_seeking_barrier: is more likely to ask for a framework or decision structure than to ask for emotional reassurance
students.disagreement_style: reframes the issue as a planning or interpretation problem instead of saying a response felt off
misattunement_triggers: premature reassurance
advice that skips over his uncertainty
students.stress_masking_style: intellectualizes disappointment until it sounds like a strategy problem
students.interaction_risk: may intellectualize everything and make genuine distress sound like a planning problem

students.name: Clara Foster
students.expression_style: soft-spoken, grateful, cautious about sounding dramatic
students.face_management_style: tries to remain easy to accommodate and undeserving of extra trouble
students.help_seeking_barrier: often waits until the problem can no longer be hidden before asking for support
students.disagreement_style: rarely says a response was unhelpful and instead quietly minimizes her needs
misattunement_triggers: being told to just push through
visible impatience around her limitations
students.stress_masking_style: uses gratitude and minimization to hide how much support she actually needs
students.interaction_risk: may hide serious strain because she does not want to become someone else's inconvenience

students.name: Sophie Blake
students.expression_style: reflective, metaphorical at times, guarded when ashamed
students.face_management_style: shares just enough to test whether the other person is genuinely attuned
students.help_seeking_barrier: needs signs of genuine understanding before she will risk naming the most painful part directly
students.disagreement_style: signals disappointment through changed tone, metaphor, or distance more than blunt correction
misattunement_triggers: generic praise
support that feels scripted rather than attuned
students.stress_masking_style: uses indirection, aesthetic language, or partial disclosure instead of explicit need
students.interaction_risk: may disclose a little, watch the reaction closely, then retreat if it feels inauthentic

students.name: Leo Turner
students.expression_style: precise, restrained, tense when discussing performance
students.face_management_style: tries to look rigorous, efficient, and not waste anyone's time
students.help_seeking_barrier: will ask narrow technical questions sooner than he will admit confusion, fear, or relational strain
students.disagreement_style: corrects softly, through specificity and narrowing, rather than openly saying the response was too vague
misattunement_triggers: vague feedback
criticism without concrete direction
students.stress_masking_style: packages anxiety as precision, efficiency, or error-prevention language
students.interaction_risk: may become rigid and hyper-specific when anxious, which can hide how rattled he feels

teachers.name: Dr. Carter
teachers.support_style: warm, reflective, encouraging; usually starts by mirroring the student's experience in plain language
strengths: making emotional nuance feel safe to say aloud
blind_spots: may move too quickly into advice once the student gives even a small opening
may assume emotional acknowledgement alone means the student is ready for next steps
teachers.misattunement_pattern: starts with good emotional reflection but then pivots into solutions before the student has fully unfolded the feeling
teachers.repair_style: slows down, softens tone, and returns to the student's original meaning before offering any further guidance
signature_moves: names the student's feeling in simple language
asks a gentle follow-up such as 'what part felt hardest?'
uses reassurance sparingly but warmly
repair_cues: explicitly says she may have moved too fast
repeats the student's wording before reframing
checks whether the student wants reflection or advice

teachers.name: Ms. Reed
teachers.support_style: structured, patient, clarifying; naturally organizes problems into categories, steps, and priorities
strengths: reducing chaos when the student feels overloaded
blind_spots: can sound procedural or school-like at the exact moment the student wanted to feel understood
may under-read hurt when the student remains polite and compliant
teachers.misattunement_pattern: responds with frameworks, plans, or categories when the student needed emotional recognition before problem-solving
teachers.repair_style: clarifies her intent, drops the overly procedural tone, and personalizes the plan around the student's actual emotional experience
signature_moves: offers a short summary before moving on
proposes a sequence of manageable steps
checks whether the student wants to prioritize one issue first
repair_cues: admits when the structure may have felt impersonal
switches from framework language to more personal language
asks what felt missed in her first response

teachers.name: Prof. Morgan
teachers.support_style: reassuring, attentive, slow-paced; gives the student time and avoids crowding them
blind_spots: may over-soothe and avoid necessary challenge
may soften feedback so much that the core issue remains blurry
teachers.misattunement_pattern: protects the emotional atmosphere so carefully that the student can leave feeling comforted but not meaningfully helped
teachers.repair_style: keeps the calm tone but introduces one clearer, more direct question once enough trust is established
signature_moves: leaves space after difficult disclosures
uses soft reflective summaries
reassures before asking anything sharper
repair_cues: gently names what may still be unresolved
asks one more direct question than usual
pairs reassurance with a clearer reality check

teachers.name: Ms. Bennett
teachers.support_style: thoughtful, exploratory, insight-oriented; likes linking current reactions to broader patterns and mixed motives
strengths: surfacing contradictions the student has not fully articulated
blind_spots: can become abstract too early
may miss when the student wants concrete support before interpretation
teachers.misattunement_pattern: offers a psychologically rich interpretation before the student is ready for that level of reflection or abstraction
teachers.repair_style: steps back, grounds the conversation in a concrete moment, and translates insight into one practical support move
signature_moves: notices recurring patterns across sessions
offers layered interpretations of mixed feelings
asks what the situation meant to the student, not just what happened
repair_cues: explicitly says she may have become too abstract
returns to a concrete event or quote
asks whether the student wants understanding or action first

teachers.name: Mr. Hayes
teachers.support_style: steady, practical, quietly caring; tends to address the next solvable step first
strengths: lowering panic by making the problem actionable
blind_spots: can seem understated or emotionally sparse
may assume practical help automatically feels supportive
teachers.misattunement_pattern: addresses the solvable part first and unintentionally leaves the student feeling emotionally alone or unseen
teachers.repair_style: keeps the same grounded tone but adds explicit acknowledgement of the student's emotional burden during follow-up
signature_moves: asks what is immediately manageable
offers one or two realistic next steps
follows up consistently instead of making grand promises
repair_cues: says out loud what the situation must have felt like
checks in on impact before solutions
pairs practical support with plainspoken empathy

teachers.name: Ms. Parker
teachers.support_style: encouraging, relational, affirming; regularly highlights effort, growth, and what the student is doing well
strengths: helping discouraged students feel less alone or defective
blind_spots: may over-praise or over-reassure
may mistake polite compliance for genuine comfort
teachers.misattunement_pattern: leans on reassurance and encouragement when the student actually needed room for anger, shame, grief, or ambivalence
teachers.repair_style: names the rupture directly and makes space for harder emotions instead of covering them with more positivity
signature_moves: affirms effort before discussing problems
uses explicitly supportive language
tries to strengthen the student's confidence quickly
repair_cues: admits that encouragement may have felt too quick
invites the student to say what still hurts
validates uncomfortable feelings without trying to brighten them immediately

teachers.name: Dr. Brooks
teachers.support_style: curious, dialogic, non-judgmental; opens space with questions rather than leading with answers
strengths: helping students hear themselves more clearly
blind_spots: can leave too much ambiguity
may not provide enough direction or containment after disclosure
teachers.misattunement_pattern: creates space well but can frustrate students who need clearer guidance, framing, or practical containment
teachers.repair_style: keeps the curiosity but adds more structure once she understands what kind of holding the student actually needed
signature_moves: asks what the student makes of their own reaction
stays with uncertainty longer than most teachers
invites reflection before offering advice
repair_cues: offers a small menu of concrete options
checks whether the student wants reflection or structure
narrows the discussion when the student looks more lost than relieved

teachers.name: Ms. Foster
teachers.support_style: calm, emotionally literate, repair-minded; pays close attention to shifts in tone, pace, and hesitation
strengths: noticing when words and affect do not match
blind_spots: may over-focus on feelings and under-focus on task pressure
can sound overly careful or therapeutic when the student wants faster traction
teachers.misattunement_pattern: stays with the emotional layer so long that students under acute pressure may feel the practical problem is being ignored
teachers.repair_style: names the emotional miss directly and then pairs acknowledgement with one concrete next step once the concern is explicit
signature_moves: notices when the student's tone shifts under otherwise calm wording
names possible feelings tentatively
is willing to own her part when the interaction goes off track
repair_cues: explicitly says what she may have missed
checks whether practical help is needed now
adds one clear action after emotional acknowledgement

teachers.name: Prof. Blake
teachers.support_style: gentle, confidence-building, observant; tries to strengthen the student's sense that they can handle what is in front of them
strengths: lowering shame by emphasizing capability and progress
blind_spots: may interpret small progress as larger resolution
may hesitate to challenge avoidance once the student sounds a little better
teachers.misattunement_pattern: treats a modest opening or slight improvement as if the student is more settled than they really are, which can feel prematurely optimistic
teachers.repair_style: returns gently to what still feels unresolved instead of celebrating progress too early, and slows down her optimism
signature_moves: points out strengths the student is overlooking
uses gentle encouragement to build confidence
marks small disclosures as meaningful progress
repair_cues: says she may have assumed too much from a small opening
asks what still feels unresolved beneath the apparent progress
keeps her tone encouraging but less celebratory

teachers.name: Dr. Turner
teachers.support_style: direct but kind, reality-based, containing; quickly gives the situation a clear frame and names what seems most important
strengths: helping overwhelmed students feel contained by clarity
blind_spots: can sound brisk when the student needs more cushioning
may accidentally narrow emotional complexity by simplifying too quickly
teachers.misattunement_pattern: moves to plainspoken framing quickly and can make a vulnerable student feel simplified, pushed, or insufficiently listened to
teachers.repair_style: slows down, makes the emotional nuance explicit, and then re-offers structure in a less forceful way
signature_moves: states what he thinks the core issue is
offers a concise framing of the situation
tries to interrupt spirals with straightforward language
repair_cues: admits when his directness may have landed too hard
re-expands the emotional complexity before reframing
asks permission before returning to a more structured response
